summary: A stellar soundboard capture of a high‑energy late‑1971 show featuring a smoking Dark Star, soaring Lovelight jam and Keith’s piano on fire.
review: The Charlie Miller transfer delivers crystal‑clear soundboard quality that lets every instrument shine – even if Keith sometimes dominates the mix and Phil sits a little low. Musically the concert is a peak‑era performance with Dark Star, Lovelight, Mr. Charlie, China>Rider and a blistering Run Rudolph Run among other highlights. Overall fans rave about the energy, tight playing and memorable jams despite minor mixing quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (40+) give 5‑star praise for both performance and sound; a few note mix imbalances, but the show is repeatedly called one of the best of ’71, yielding a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally superb clarity and balance; occasional left‑channel buzz and an overly prominent Keith piano/organ, with Phil’s bass slightly recessed. Overall one of the best‑sounding board recordings from this era.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows with relentless energy – tight first‑set playing leads into a second set that builds through Dark Star into a seamless series of jams, showcasing the band’s chemistry and dynamic transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised repeatedly for soaring tone, expressive solos and overall leadership; many call his playing ‘on fire’ or ‘sky‑high’.
band_member_comments.Phil: A few reviewers note Phil’s bass is buried in the mix, but acknowledge his solid foundation when audible.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals described as terrific; his presence is clear despite occasional mix issues.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano/organ is a focal point – lauded for being ‘hot’, prominent and full of energy, though some feel it overpowers the rest of the band.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming is generally well‑received; minor comments about cymbal prominence but no major criticisms.
